# Clade Reference: Symbolic Line — v0.3

*Working document. Do not cite externally.*

## How to read this document

Each entry covers one cell of the symbolic line in the ABHOP framework. Venn labels (motivations, tendencies, signatures, capacities, breakthrough) are pulled verbatim from *Inheritance Lines v3.0* and must not be paraphrased. The constituent-human framing names the individual parameterized by collective context, not the collective itself; the collective is symbolic-fauna, the personality traits are symbolic-flora.

**Traceability marks:**

- **HIGH** — full APA confirmed in Smithers extraction or SQLite cache; wording verified
- **MODERATE** — partial details or reconstructed from extraction text; APA not fully confirmed
- **LOW** — secondary source or unverified chain
- **[INFERRED]** — no Smithers extraction source; general knowledge only

---

## Cell 1: Band-Human

**What the cell is.** A Band-Human is a human whose personality constellation is parameterized by band life: a mobile forager group of roughly 20–30 adults whose cohesion depends entirely on direct reciprocal exchange among individuals who are mostly not close genetic relatives. The breaking motivation at this cell is Reciprocity (Effector slot), which displaces Boldness — marking the neuronal-to-symbolic boundary and making reputation management the primary individual imperative. The collective this human inhabits is the band as meme-fauna; the personality traits expressed are symbolic-flora calibrated to band ecology.

**The milestone.** Speaking — the capacity for open-ended, combinatorially productive language that makes gossip, reputation tracking, and shared intentionality scalable beyond dyadic grooming ranges. Behavioral modernity markers in *Homo sapiens* cluster around 100–300 KYA; the framework treats Speaking as the breakthrough that makes Reciprocity operationalizable at band scale. Note: Neanderthal symbolic evidence (see Open tension below) complicates a sapiens-specific dating.

**Key references.**

| Citation | Traceability | Relevance |
|---|---|---|
| Hill, K. R., Walker, R. S., Božičević, M., Eder, J., Headland, T., Hewlett, B., Hurtado, A. M., Marlowe, F., Wiessner, P., & Wood, B. (2011). Co-residence patterns in hunter-gatherer societies show unique human social structure. *Science*, *331*(6022), 1286–1289. | HIGH | Mean band size ~28 adults across 32 societies; co-residents are mostly genetically unrelated — establishes Reciprocity-over-kinship as the structural norm, not an exception |
| Walker, R. S., Flinn, M. V., & Hill, K. R. (2010). Evolutionary history of partible paternity in lowland South America. *Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences*, *107*(45), 19195–19200. | MODERATE | Reciprocal exogamy and Intergroup Marriage as systematic social technology extending coalition radius beyond any single band's fighting strength |
| Kaplan, H., Hill, K., Lancaster, J., & Hurtado, A. M. (2000). A theory of human life history evolution: Diet, intelligence, and longevity. *Evolutionary Anthropology*, *9*(4), 156–185. | HIGH | Band-level Meat Pooling as primary Reactor capacity; embodied capital theory; forager sharing as reciprocal intergenerational investment across skill-acquisition time horizons |
| Henshilwood, C. S., d'Errico, F., van Niekerk, K. L., Jacobs, Z., Lauritzen, S.-E., & Watts, I. (2018). An abstract drawing from the 73,000-year-old levels at Blombos Cave, South Africa. *Nature*, *562*, 115–118. | HIGH | Earliest confirmed abstract mark-making; primary chronological anchor for the Speaking breakthrough |
| Henshilwood, C. S., d'Errico, F., & Watts, I. (2009). Engraved ochres from the Middle Stone Age levels at Blombos Cave, South Africa. *Journal of Human Evolution*, *57*(1), 27–47. | MODERATE | Engraved geometric ochre pieces 75–100 KYA; partial confirmation of symbolic milestone date range |
| Tennie, C., Call, J., & Tomasello, M. (2009). Ratcheting up the ratchet: On the evolution of cumulative culture. *Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B*, *364*(1528), 2405–2415. | HIGH | Cumulative culture mechanism — true imitation + teaching = ratchet; structurally necessary for the Common Knowledge Planner capacity |

**Fun specifics.**

- Hill et al. (2011) sampled 32 hunter-gatherer societies and found that median co-resident band members were mostly *not* close genetic relatives — cohesion runs on Reciprocity, not kinship alone, which makes the Effector slot shift from Boldness to Reciprocity structurally load-bearing rather than ornamental (Hill et al., 2011).
- Kaplan et al. (2000) show that a forager hunter delivers roughly 40–60% of calories consumed by others across his lifetime, making Meat Pooling a net subsidy from skilled adults to dependents that only makes sense under a reciprocal accounting system that persists across decades (Kaplan et al., 2000).
- Intergroup Marriage (Conservator capacity) is not merely a kinship nicety: Walker et al. (2010) document reciprocal exogamy systems that systematically extend coalition radius beyond any single band's fighting strength — reproductive exchange as political alliance infrastructure.
- Blombos Cave preserves a cross-hatch drawing on silcrete at 73 KYA (Henshilwood et al., 2018) and engraved geometric ochre pieces at 75–100 KYA (Henshilwood et al., 2009); both predate any verbal record of Speaking, making material traces the only chronological anchor for the breakthrough.
- Self-Reference (Connector signature) implies the band-level individual can hold a stable representation of how *others* represent *them* — the cognitive groundwork for shame, pride, and reputation monitoring; all three self-conscious emotions presuppose a theory of mind that models one's own model of oneself. [INFERRED — no Smithers extraction covers Cooley, Tangney, Tracy & Robins, or Hofstadter directly]
- Tennie et al. (2009) distinguish individual reinvention (great apes' ceiling) from ratcheted accumulation (human floor): once Speaking is available, Common Knowledge can be stored outside any single head and retrieved across generations without recapitulating the discovery — the cognitive logic of the Planner slot.

**Smithers coverage.** MODERATE-to-HIGH for forager ecology. The Innovations field reconnaissance extraction (Block 2) covers band size, meat pooling, and intergroup marriage with full APA for Hill et al. (2011) and Kaplan et al. (2000) and partial confirmation for Walker et al. (2010). Block 4 provides Henshilwood et al. (2018) at HIGH and (2009) at MODERATE; Tennie et al. (2009) appears in extraction text. ABSENT for the Self-Reference / self-conscious emotion cluster (Cooley, Tangney, Tracy & Robins, Hofstadter) — all [INFERRED] — and for the gossip literature (Dunbar) and Boehm's reverse-dominance hierarchy work.

**Open tension.** The Innovations extraction documents Neanderthal symbolic behavior — shell ornaments at ~115 KYA (Hoffmann et al., 2018), Iberian cave art at >64.8 KYA (Hoffmann et al., 2018), Bruniquel Cave ring structures at 176.5 KYA (Jaubert et al., 2016) — predating or flanking the sapiens-centered 100 KYA milestone. If these constitute Speaking precursors, the Band-Human milestone date and species-specificity require revision. The extraction notes explicitly that "claims of symbolic uniqueness for *Homo sapiens* are no longer defensible in their strong form." The framework should adjudicate whether Speaking is sapiens-specific or genus-level before this cell is used for book passages.

---

## Cell 2: Settlement-Human

**What the cell is.** A Settlement-Human is a human whose personality constellation is parameterized by sedentary village life: a fixed-location community whose cohesion depends on accumulation, storage, and recurring structured obligation rather than mobile reciprocal exchange. The breaking motivation at this cell is Stability (Regulator slot), which displaces Mastery — shifting the individual's primary orientation from skill-demonstration to consistent rule-following across seasons. The collective this human inhabits is the settlement as a norm-generating superorganism; the personality traits expressed are symbolic-flora calibrated to sedentary agricultural ecology.

**The milestone.** Norms — shared behavioral expectations that hold across individuals who are not watching each other, making large anonymous coordination possible for the first time without enforcement from a central authority. The Neolithic Revolution provides the archaeological anchor: cultivation begins in the Fertile Crescent ~11,700–10,200 BP; Çatalhöyük (~9,500–7,700 BP) represents a large sedentary agglomeration without clear hierarchy, operating on normative coordination alone. [Milestone dating [INFERRED] — no Smithers extraction covers Neolithic archaeology directly.]

**Key references.**

| Citation | Traceability | Relevance |
|---|---|---|
| Roberts, B. W. (2006). Continuity and change in personality from childhood to adulthood. In D. K. Mroczek & T. D. Little (Eds.), *Handbook of personality development* (pp. 51–70). Lawrence Erlbaum Associates. | MODERATE | Social Investment Principle: adult role commitment creates environmental press for Conscientiousness crystallization — mechanism for why Settlement-Human produces the Conscientiousness debut at the Effector×Regulator Controller slot |
| Roberts, B. W., Kuncel, N. R., Shiner, R., Caspi, A., & Goldberg, L. R. (2007). The power of personality: The comparative validity of personality traits, socioeconomic status, and cognitive ability for predicting important life outcomes. *Perspectives on Psychological Science*, *2*(4), 313–345. | MODERATE | Conscientiousness predicts mortality r=.09, larger than SES (r=.02) or IQ (r=.06); approximately 7 additional years of life expectancy — quantifies the survival stakes of the Conscientiousness slot in agricultural context |
| Roberts, B. W., Jackson, J. J., Fayard, J. V., Edmonds, G., & Meints, J. (2012). Conscientiousness. In M. R. Leary & R. H. Hoyle (Eds.), *Handbook of individual differences in social behavior* (pp. 369–381). Guilford Press. | MODERATE | Industriousness (sustained effort toward goals) vs. Orderliness (routine maintenance of environment) bifurcation within Conscientiousness; maps onto Farming (Planner) vs. Householding (Conservator) distinction |
| Stephan, Y., Sutin, A. R., & Terracciano, A. (2018). Subjective age and personality development: A 10-year study. *Journal of Personality*, *86*(3), 413–423. | MODERATE | Industriousness facet predicts longevity (~25% lower mortality per SD); self-control facet does not — facet-level specificity relevant to the agricultural-commitment interpretation |

**Fun specifics.**

- Çatalhöyük (~9,500–7,700 BP) housed up to 8,000 people with no evident palace, temple, or elite burial; every house is the same size and entered through the roof, making architectural equality literally structurally enforced — the settlement ran on norms rather than hierarchy, which is exactly the coordination logic the Norms breakthrough names. [INFERRED — no Smithers extraction covers Çatalhöyük]
- Roberts et al. (2007) show that Conscientiousness predicts survival outcomes more strongly than IQ (r=.06) or socioeconomic status (r=.02); in a sedentary agricultural setting where stored food is the margin between survival and starvation across seasons, the individual who shows up reliably to plant, weed, and harvest has a categorical adaptive advantage over the skilled improviser (Roberts et al., 2007).
- Roberts et al. (2012) show that Conscientiousness bifurcates into Industriousness (keep working) and Orderliness (keep things in their place); the settlement household requires both facets in a way band foraging does not — crops do not wait for the hunter to feel ready, and grain stores require systematic maintenance.
- Self-Construal (Operator signature, debut at Settlement-Human) captures the independent-to-interdependent dimension of self-representation; the shift from Band-Human's Personality to Settlement-Human's Self-Construal marks the cultural individuation/embeddedness axis becoming salient as fixed property rights and household boundaries create the first stable structures around which independent vs. embedded selfhood can crystallize. [INFERRED for Self-Construal archaeological correlate — no Smithers extraction covers Markus & Kitayama or Triandis]
- Storing (Reactor capacity) is the structural inversion of Meat Pooling: foragers share meat because it spoils and because reciprocal sharing averages risk; settlers store grain because it keeps and because stored surplus is the founding act of unequal wealth accumulation — the same Effector Reactor slot, an inverted social logic. [INFERRED]
- Assertiveness (Regulator×Modeler Anticipator) at Settlement-Human functions as norm-negotiating rather than dominance-asserting: the settled farmer establishes position within a stable hierarchy through reliable performance and vocal claim-making, not open competition with strangers. [INFERRED — no extraction covers Assertiveness as distinct from Extraversion in this context]

**Smithers coverage.** DENSE for the Conscientiousness psychology sub-cluster: the Conscientiousness source card covers Roberts (2006), Roberts et al. (2007), Roberts et al. (2012), and Stephan et al. (2018) at MODERATE traceability, providing functional grounding for the Controller debut. ABSENT for Neolithic archaeology (Çatalhöyük, Göbekli Tepe, Fertile Crescent chronology), Self-Construal literature (Markus & Kitayama, Triandis), and Assertiveness as a distinct construct. A Settlement-Human book passage requires [INFERRED] scaffolding for all archaeological and self-construal components.

---

## Cell 3: City-Human

**What the cell is.** A City-Human is a human whose personality constellation is parameterized by urban life: a dense, occupationally differentiated settlement whose cohesion depends on legal codes, market exchange, and institutional representation rather than face-to-face normative pressure. The breaking motivation at this cell is Representation (Modeler slot), which displaces Affiliation — shifting the individual's primary orientation from belonging to a face-to-face community toward being recognized within an institutional order that includes strangers. The collective this human inhabits is the city as a symbiosis of settlements and trade networks, making this the first symbiosis cell on the symbolic line; the personality traits expressed are symbolic-flora calibrated to urban anonymous ecology.

**The milestone.** Codes — written, portable, enforceable rule-sets that coordinate strangers who share no face-to-face norm-maintenance history. The Urban Revolution provides the archaeological anchor: Uruk (~5,500–5,000 BP), Mohenjo-daro (~4,500–3,900 BP), and contemporaneous Nile and Yellow River urban centers represent the first settlements large enough that codes rather than norms must bear the primary coordination load. [Milestone dating [INFERRED] — no Smithers extraction covers city archaeology directly.]

**Key references.**

| Citation | Traceability | Relevance |
|---|---|---|
| Judge, T. A., Locke, E. A., Durham, C. C., & Kluger, A. N. (1998). Dispositional effects on job and life satisfaction: The role of core evaluations. *Journal of Applied Psychology*, *83*(1), 17–34. | HIGH | Founding Core Self-Evaluations paper; Self-Evaluation (Connector signature, debut at City-Human) operationalized as self-esteem + generalized self-efficacy + locus of control + emotional stability |
| Judge, T. A., & Bono, J. E. (2001). Relationship of core self-evaluations traits — self-esteem, generalized self-efficacy, locus of control, and emotional stability — with job satisfaction and job performance: A meta-analysis. *Journal of Applied Psychology*, *86*(1), 80–92. | HIGH | Meta-analytic confirmation of the CSE cluster across 169 independent samples (N~59,000); ρ=.37 for job satisfaction, ρ=.23 for job performance |
| Judge, T. A., Bono, J. E., Erez, A., & Locke, E. A. (2005). Core self-evaluations and job and life satisfaction: The role of self-concordance and goal attainment. *Journal of Applied Psychology*, *90*(2), 257–268. | HIGH | CSE mediates outcome effects via goal self-concordance — maps onto city-scale occupational role structure where institutional position is the primary self-evaluation referent |
| Ashton, M. C., & Lee, K. (2007). Empirical, theoretical, and practical advantages of the HEXACO model of personality structure. *Personality and Social Psychology Review*, *11*(2), 150–166. | MODERATE | Righteousness (Adapter at City-Human, debut here before Integrity at Empire) framed within HEXACO Agreeableness vs. Honesty-Humility distinction; establishes the moral-emotions Adapter progression |

**Fun specifics.**

- The Sumerian cuneiform tablets at Uruk (~5,200 BP) are primarily accounting documents — lists of beer rations and grain allocations — not literature; the first writing is Codes, not poetry, and the first urban personality problem is who gets what from whom according to what enforceable rule. [INFERRED — no Smithers extraction covers Mesopotamian archaeology]
- Judge et al. (1998) define Core Self-Evaluations as a unitary higher-order construct binding self-esteem, generalized self-efficacy, locus of control, and emotional stability; in a city, all four collapse to a single question: can I hold my institutional position against strangers who are also competing for it? Self-Evaluation replaces Self-Reference because the reference group is now an institution, not a face-to-face band (Judge et al., 1998).
- Extraversion (Regulator×Modeler Anticipator, debut at City-Human) emerges structurally as the city creates an occupational ecology in which asserting oneself in front of strangers pays — the merchant, the lawyer, the priest, the soldier are all roles requiring social approach behavior with unrelated others, a context absent in bands and uncommon in settlements. [INFERRED for ecological correlate; trait debut from *Inheritance Lines v3.0*]
- Crime (Pursuer capacity, debut at City-Human) is not pathology but structure: the city creates sufficient surplus, anonymity, and institutional complexity that defecting on codes becomes a viable individual life strategy for the first time; the Pursuer slot tracks the individual whose goal-seeking is code-violating rather than code-compliant. [INFERRED]
- Judge & Bono (2001) meta-analyzed 169 independent samples and found CSE correlates ρ=.37 with job satisfaction across occupational contexts — city-scale life produces stable enough occupational roles that personality variation on the CSE cluster becomes a reliable predictor of whether an individual thrives within the institutional order or chafes against it (Judge & Bono, 2001).
- Righteousness (Adapter at City-Human) is the first appearance of a moral-identity trait in the Adapter slot: Agreeableness (cooperation toward the proximal known other) gives way to Righteousness (alignment with the city's codes as moral authority), marking the transition from relationship-maintenance to norm-internalization as the primary individual accommodation mechanism. [INFERRED for mechanism; label from *Inheritance Lines v3.0*]

**Smithers coverage.** MODERATE-HIGH for the Core Self-Evaluations cluster: Judge et al. (1998), Judge & Bono (2001), and Judge et al. (2005) are all confirmed at HIGH traceability in the SQLite cache (`papers` table), making Self-Evaluation (Connector) the best-documented structural element in this cell. ABSENT for city archaeology, urban scaling theory (West, Bettencourt), the anthropology of trade networks, Extraversion's ecological emergence story, and the structural sociology of Crime as a city-specific life strategy. The Extraversion debut has no extraction support beyond the *Inheritance Lines* framework label itself.

---

## Cell 4: Empire-Human

**What the cell is.** An Empire-Human is a human whose personality constellation is parameterized by imperial life: a multi-city polity whose cohesion depends on shared Stories — origin myths, sacred texts, ideological systems, and dynastic narratives — rather than face-to-face codes or norms. The breaking motivation at this cell is Universality (Reviser slot), which displaces Autonomy — shifting the individual's primary orientation from personal freedom-within-codes toward participation in a collective narrative that makes the empire's reach feel cosmically legitimate. The collective this human inhabits is the empire as a symbiosis of cities and colonial outposts; the personality traits expressed are symbolic-flora calibrated to an ecology where abstract identification with a vast, impersonal order is the primary individual survival problem.

**The milestone.** Stories — ideologically coherent, transmissible narratives that make empire-scale identification feel natural rather than coerced. The Axial Age (~800–200 BCE) provides the clearest crystallization point: the near-simultaneous emergence of Confucianism, Buddhism, Greek philosophy, Zoroastrianism, and Second Temple Judaism across four independent civilizational cores each produce universal ethical frameworks capable of motivating strangers to identify with an imperial order beyond their immediate city-state. [Milestone dating [INFERRED] from general knowledge and Jaspers (1949); no Smithers extraction covers the Axial Age directly.]

**Key references.**

| Citation | Traceability | Relevance |
|---|---|---|
| De Raad, B., Barelds, D. P. H., Levert, E., Ostendorf, F., Mlačić, B., Di Blas, L., Hřebíčková, M., Szirmák, Z., Szarota, P., Perugini, M., Church, A. T., & Katigbak, M. S. (2010). Only three factors of personality description are fully replicable across languages: A comparison of 14 trait taxonomies. *Journal of Personality and Social Psychology*, *98*(1), 160–173. | HIGH | Openness-to-Experience does NOT replicate cross-linguistically; only Dynamism, Affiliation, and Order do — supports the framework's prediction that Intellect is a late-arriving empire-context trait rather than a pan-human baseline |
| De Raad, B., Barelds, D. P. H., Timmerman, M. E., De Roover, K., Mlačić, B., & Church, A. T. (2014). Towards a pan-cultural personality structure: Input from 11 psycholexical studies. *European Journal of Personality*, *28*(5), 497–510. | HIGH | Replication of the three-factor solution across 11 language groups; confirms Openness/Intellect as non-universal, consistent with its appearance only at the terminal symbolic-line cell |
| Ashton, M. C., & Lee, K. (2007). Empirical, theoretical, and practical advantages of the HEXACO model of personality structure. *Personality and Social Psychology Review*, *11*(2), 150–166. | MODERATE | HEXACO Honesty-Humility as framework for Integrity (Adapter debut at Empire-Human); evolutionary distinction between kin-altruism (Agreeableness) and reciprocal-altruism (H-H) grounds the Adapter trajectory from Agreeableness → Righteousness → Integrity |
| Ashton, M. C., Lee, K., & de Vries, R. E. (2014). The HEXACO Honesty-Humility, Agreeableness, and Emotionality factors: A review of research and theory. *Personality and Social Psychology Review*, *18*(2), 139–152. | MODERATE | Honesty-Humility is the strongest HEXACO predictor of unethical behavior and dark personality measures; Integrity as individual adaptation to empire-scale enforcement environments where defection is both more tempting and more visible |
| Assmann, J., & Czaplicka, J. (1995). Collective memory and cultural identity. *New German Critique*, (65), 125–133. | HIGH | Cultural memory vs. communicative memory distinction — the Stories breakthrough operationalized: cultural memory exceeds any living person's recall horizon and is the groundwork for empire-scale identification |
| Assmann, J. (2011). Communicative and cultural memory. In P. Meusburger, M. Heffernan, & E. Wunder (Eds.), *Cultural memories: The geographical point of view* (Knowledge and Space, Vol. 4, pp. 15–27). Springer. | HIGH | Elaborated framework for cultural memory as the cognitive infrastructure of imperial identification; Universality motivation requires this cognitive form — identification with a community one cannot directly perceive |

**Fun specifics.**

- De Raad et al. (2010) compared 14 personality taxonomies across 12 language groups and found only three factors replicate with high congruence: Dynamism (Extraversion-like), Affiliation (Agreeableness-like), and Order (Conscientiousness-like); Openness-to-Experience failed to replicate — exactly what the framework predicts if Intellect is a late-arriving trait whose ecological conditions (scribal culture, philosophical inquiry, multi-civilization contact) only assemble at imperial scale (De Raad et al., 2010).
- Assmann & Czaplicka (1995) distinguish *communicative memory* (living conversation, ~80-year horizon, biographical and personal) from *cultural memory* (sacred texts, rituals, monuments, multi-generational and institutionally maintained); the Stories breakthrough is precisely this transition — empire cohesion requires cultural memory because the empire outlasts any communicative chain (Assmann & Czaplicka, 1995).
- Ashton & Lee (2016) report that Honesty-Humility increases approximately 1 SD from age 18 to 60 in a sample of N~97,583 — Integrity is a late-life crystallization within individual development, consistent with its position as the most structurally recent Adapter slot on the symbolic line. [Ashton & Lee 2016 cited from HEXACO source card; full APA not confirmed — MODERATE]
- Identity (Operator signature, debut at Empire-Human) replaces Self-Construal: the individual is no longer navigating independent vs. interdependent self-representation relative to a known community but identifying themselves as Roman, Han, Muslim, or Brahmin — categories that are cosmological rather than face-to-face; the referent community is imagined and the self-label is imperial. [INFERRED for mechanism; label from *Inheritance Lines v3.0*]
- Conquering (Reactor capacity) is the imperial-scale inversion of Meat Pooling: both occupy the same Effector Reactor slot, both track the individual's primary accumulation behavior, but where forager accumulation is dietary sharing that averages risk, imperial accumulation is territorial absorption that concentrates it — the same structural position, an inverted social logic. [INFERRED]
- Empire-Human is the only symbolic-line cell to carry a secondary personality disorder panel in the framework, reflecting that the Universality motivation, when it fails, produces characteristic decompensation profiles not seen at earlier cells; this makes Empire-Human the implicit referent for much of clinical personality taxonomy. [Framework observation from *Inheritance Lines v3.0*; no extraction source]

**Smithers coverage.** MODERATE for personality anchors. De Raad et al. (2010, 2014) confirmed HIGH in SQLite, providing the strongest empirical grounding for the Intellect (non-universal Openness) claim. Assmann (1995, 2011) confirmed HIGH in SQLite, grounding the Stories milestone in cultural memory theory. HEXACO literature (Ashton & Lee 2007; Ashton et al. 2014) at MODERATE provides the Integrity anchor. ABSENT for empire history, Axial Age scholarship (Jaspers, Bellah, Armstrong), social identity theory (Tajfel & Turner), and the imagined communities literature (Anderson, 1983) — all necessary for an empirically grounded Empire-Human book passage.

---

## Cross-cutting notes

### Big Five accretion across the symbolic line

| Slot | Band-Human | Settlement-Human | City-Human | Empire-Human |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Effector×Regulator Controller | Standing | **Conscientiousness** *(debut)* | Conscientiousness | Conscientiousness |
| Regulator×Modeler Anticipator | Experiencing Style | Assertiveness | **Extraversion** *(debut)* | Extraversion |
| Modeler×Reviser Adapter | Agreeableness | Agreeableness | Righteousness | **Integrity** *(debut)* |
| Reviser×Effector Explorer | Neuroticism | Neuroticism | Neuroticism | **Intellect** *(debut, replaces Neuroticism)* |
| Effector∩Modeler Connector | Self-Reference | Self-Reference | **Self-Evaluation** *(debut)* | Self-Evaluation |
| Regulator∩Reviser Operator | Personality | **Self-Construal** *(debut)* | Self-Construal | **Identity** *(debut)* |

Openness-to-Experience has no structural slot on the symbolic line. Its psycholexical non-replication (De Raad et al., 2010, 2014) is consistent with its omission.

### Breakthrough progression

Speaking (Band) → Norms (Settlement) → Codes (City) → Stories (Empire)

Each breakthrough extends the radius of coordination: face-to-face band → multi-household village → anonymous city → imagined civilizational community.

### Symbiosis vs. elaboration

- **Band → Settlement:** elaboration (mobile band + storage = settled village)
- **Settlement → City:** symbiosis (settlements + trade networks = city)
- **City → Empire:** symbiosis (cities + colonial outposts = empire)

### Constituent-human type progression

Band-Human forager → Settlement-Human sedentary farmer → City-Human occupational-role urban dweller → Empire-Human imagined-community imperial subject

### Escalation items (surfaced before writing; not adjudicated here)

1. **Neanderthal symbolic evidence:** Extraction documents Neanderthal symbolic behavior (Bruniquel 176.5 KYA; Iberian cave art >64.8 KYA; shell ornaments ~115 KYA) predating or flanking the sapiens-centered 100 KYA Band-Human milestone. Framework should adjudicate whether Speaking is sapiens-specific or genus-level.
2. **Self-Reference cluster absent:** No Smithers extraction covers Cooley, Lewis, Tangney, Tracy & Robins, or Hofstadter — the cognitive infrastructure for Band-Human's Connector signature is entirely [INFERRED].
3. **Neolithic archaeology absent:** No extraction covers Çatalhöyük, Göbekli Tepe, or Fertile Crescent chronology — all Settlement-Human milestone material is [INFERRED].
4. **Axial Age absent:** No extraction covers Jaspers, Bellah, Armstrong, or Anderson — Empire-Human milestone material and imagined-communities logic are [INFERRED].

---

## Intellectual Kinships

This section records thinkers, traditions, and bodies of work that the framework absorbs or converges with at the symbolic line. Two patterns organize the entries. **Absorption** means the thinker's vocabulary enters the Venns directly as the canonical content for a specific region. **Convergence** means the thinker arrives at a compatible structural commitment by an independent route without shared vocabulary.

### Absorptions

**Harari.** Yuval Noah Harari. *Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind* (2011). Narrative collective identity as the mechanism by which large-scale human cooperation becomes possible across kin and tribal boundaries. ABHOP places Stories at the Empire breakthrough, where Harari's framing names what empires can do that lower symbolic cells cannot.

**Social Identity (Tajfel & Turner; Erikson; McAdams; Goffman).** Henri Tajfel and John Turner, social identity theory (1979 onward). Erik Erikson, identity-formation work (1950 onward). Dan McAdams, narrative identity. Erving Goffman, *The Presentation of Self in Everyday Life* (1959). ABHOP absorbs these as the primary anchors of Identity, the signature at the Empire-Human R2×R4 pair (Operator axis). Identity is the social-science synthesis of who the person takes themselves to be within a universalizing order. It couples Stability with Universality — the empire-level Operator signature.

**Jonathan Haidt.** Moral Foundations Theory (Haidt and Joseph, 2004 onward; *The Righteous Mind*, 2012). Six moral foundations (Care, Fairness, Loyalty, Authority, Sanctity, Liberty) as the cross-cultural foundation of moral cognition. ABHOP absorbs MFT at the Empire-Human R2×R4 Operator signature, Identity. MFT operationalizes the universal moral codes empires encode. The R4-Universality flavor of the six foundations is what places MFT at the Operator (agency) rather than the Connector (communion) axis at this level.

**Boyd & Richerson / Henrich.** Robert Boyd, Peter Richerson, Joseph Henrich. Dual inheritance theory. *Not by Genes Alone* (2005). Joseph Henrich's *The Secret of Our Success* (2015) and *The WEIRDest People in the World* (2020). Dual inheritance theory converges on ABHOP's line architecture: genetic plus symbolic plus a distinct cultural inheritance modality with its own selection dynamics. Henrich's WEIRD/kinship work enters the Empire-Stack treatment: monotheism dissolving clan loyalties, cousin-marriage bans, and individualism as a civilization-scale identity solution.

**Deacon (*Symbolic Species*).** Terrence Deacon. *The Symbolic Species* (1997). Pre-symbolic indexical and iconic reference and the symbolic transition. ABHOP absorbs Deacon's pre-symbolic reference work as the symbolic-line R0 — the semiotic groundwork that the symbolic line's founding constellation installs before the Band-Human cell opens the culturally transmitted inheritance stream. Deacon's pre-biotic ratchets (*Incomplete Nature*, 2012) are absorbed separately at the Protocell; see `clade_reference_genetic_v0_2.md`.

### Convergences

**Pinker.** Steven Pinker. *The Better Angels of Our Nature* (2011) converges on the framework's symbolic-line developmental story: violence declines as the symbolic line builds increasingly comprehensive normative coordination structures from band to empire. *The Blank Slate* (2002) identifies three myths about human nature (Blank Slate, Noble Savage, Ghost in the Machine); ABHOP's fallacy taxonomy uses these three plus a fourth Pinker does not name (Dealt Hand). *The Language Instinct* (1994) converges on ABHOP's neuronal-line commitment to language as a biological substrate rather than a cultural overlay.

---

*End of document. v0.3 — 2026-05-20. Intellectual Kinships section incorporates `we_think_so_too_v0_1.md` (symbolic-line entries).*
